entries.summary: PG-002: Migration runner with dual SQLite+Pg CI support - need to create a migration runner that detects dialect (SQLite vs PG) based on OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL env var, runs Drizzle migrations against the selected dialect, supports both SQLite .sql.ts and PG .pg.sql.ts schema files, and includes CI matrix configuration for both dialects
Current drizzle.config.ts only targets SQLite with migration/ output folder. No drizzle.config.pg.ts exists. PG migration folder (migration-pg/) has 1 migration already.
db.pg.ts has applyMigrations() that supports both PGlite (in-memory/file) and node-postgres (remote), using duck-type detection on $client.exec()
adapter.ts defaultLayer already checks DatabaseConfig - if OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set, uses PG, otherwise SQLite
DatabaseConfig.ts reads OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL, OPENCODE_DATABASE_SSL, OPENCODE_DATABASE_POOL_SIZE env vars
test/fixture/pg.ts provides pgTestLayer that auto-detects PGlite vs real PG via OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL
CI (test.yml) currently only runs SQLite tests - no PG CI job exists
test:pg script runs adapter.pg.test.ts which creates tables inline (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), doesn't use applyMigrations
18 dirty files in packages/opencode/src/ from prior sessions - must be additive and preserve all changes
6 .pg.sql.ts bridge files exist (account, project, session, share, workspace, schema.pg) that re-export from .sql.ts
Migration folders use timestamp-prefixed naming: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_name/migration.sql
Existing SQLite migration tests read migration folder structure programmatically (sort by timestamp)
No CI pipeline currently runs drizzle-kit migrations or applyMigrations
